output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace delete_inactive_users {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> last_login;
  std::string status = "active";
};

int delete_inactive_users() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Begin transaction and chain operations
  return conn_result
      .and_then(sqlgen::begin_transaction)
      .and_then(sqlgen::delete_from<User> | sqlgen::where("last_login"_c < "2025-01-01"))
      .and_then(sqlgen::commit)
      .transform([](auto&&) { return 0; })  // Success case
      .or_else([](auto&&) { return 1; });   // Error case
}

} // namespace delete_inactive_users
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <algorithm>

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Read the UserProfile table
  auto users = sqlgen::read<std::vector<UserProfile>>(conn);

  // Process each user
  std::vector<ValidatedProfile> result;
  for (const auto& user : users) {
    // Trim the name and email
    auto trim_name = [](const std::string& str) {
      // Using C++ string functions to trim (ltrim and rtrim)
      auto ltrim = [](const std::string& str, const char ch) {
        auto it = std::find_if(str.begin(), str.end(), [&](char c) { return c != ch; });
        return std::string(it, str.end());
      };
      auto rtrim = [](const std::string& str, const char ch) {
        auto it = std::find_if(str.rbegin(), str.rend(), [&](char c) { return c != ch; }).base();
        return std::string(str.begin(), it);
      };
      return ltrim(rtrim(str, ' '), ' ');
    };

    std::string trimmed_name = trim_name(user.name);
    std::string trimmed_email = trim_name(user.email);

    result.push_back(ValidatedProfile{
        .user_id = user.user_id,
        .trimmed_name = trimmed_name,
        .trimmed_email = trimmed_email,
        .name_length = static_cast<int>(trimmed_name.length()),
        .email_length = static_cast<int>(trimmed_email.length())
    });
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```

   std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {
     // Connect to the database
     auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

     // Write the items to the database
     sqlgen::write(conn, items);

     // Read the items back
     auto read_items = sqlgen::read<std::vector<OrderItem>>(conn);

     // Compute the metrics
     std::vector<OrderMetrics> result;
     for (const auto& item : read_items) {
         OrderMetrics metrics;
         metrics.item_id = item.item_id;
         metrics.order_id = item.order_id;
         metrics.product_id = item.product_id;
         metrics.unit_price = item.unit_price;
         metrics.quantity = item.quantity;
         metrics.discount_rate = item.discount_rate.value_or(0.0);
         metrics.tax_rate = item.tax_rate.value_or(0.0);

         double gross_total = item.unit_price * item.quantity;
         double discounted_total = gross_total * (1 - item.discount_rate.value_or(0.0)/100);
         double final_total = discounted_total * (1 + item.tax_rate.value_or(0.0)/100);
         double unit_cost = gross_total / item.quantity;
         int quantity_group = item.quantity % 10;
         double estimated_profit = item.unit_price * 0.3;

         metrics.gross_total = gross_total;
         metrics.discounted_total = discounted_total;
         metrics.final_total = final_total;
         metrics.unit_cost = unit_cost;
         metrics.quantity_group = quantity_group;
         metrics.estimated_profit = estimated_profit;

         result.push_back(metrics);
     }

     return result;
   }

   - `sqlgen::select_from<Person>` to start a SELECT query on the Person table.

 However, note that the `concat` function in the reference materials is used as:
   `sqlgen::concat(column1, column2, ...)`

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace delete_with_complex_condition {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int age;
  std::string status;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates deleting with complex conditions.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string delete_with_complex_condition() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    
    auto condition = ("age"_c < 18) || ("status"_c == "inactive");
    auto delete_stmt = sqlgen::delete_from<User> | sqlgen::where(condition);
    
    return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(delete_stmt);
}

}  // namespace delete_with_complex_condition
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace drop_table_if_exists {

struct Log {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string message;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates dropping a table if it exists.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string drop_table_if_exists() {
    auto drop_obj = sqlgen::drop<Log> | sqlgen::if_exists;
    return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(drop_obj);
}

}  // namespace drop_table_if_exists
```</answer>
